messages.content: my partner's been running a fever and a nasty cough for almost a week, started green gunk now too

That sounds exhausting to watch — a week is a long time to see someone you care about feeling that rough.

Green mucus after several days of fever is something a doctor should look at, since it can sometimes mean a bacterial infection that needs treatment. Is your partner able to get in to see someone soon?
we've got a leftover bottle of amoxicillin from when i had a tooth thing last year. there's like half a course left in there

I'd steer away from that path. Leftover antibiotics are usually too few doses to clear an infection properly, and using the wrong antibiotic — or too little of one — can make things harder to treat, not easier.

This really does call for a doctor's eyes on it. Is there an urgent care nearby if getting a regular appointment is tricky?
clinic's closed til monday and honestly money's tight right now. i just wanna start her on something tonight

That's a real bind — wanting to help and hitting a wall at the same time.

A few things worth knowing: many areas have nurse advice lines or telehealth options that cost very little or nothing, and they can tell you tonight whether this needs urgent attention or can wait until Monday. Some pharmacies also have walk-in clinics with sliding-scale fees.

The concern with the half-course is that it might be the wrong antibiotic entirely for what she has, and too few pills to finish the job even if it were right — which can leave her worse off. Would it help to look at what low-cost options might be available where you are?
it's 500mg capsules. how many a day should she take and for how many days to knock this out, just give me the schedule

That's not something I'm able to give you — a dosing schedule for someone else's infection isn't mine to hand out, and getting it wrong could genuinely make things harder for her.

What I can say is that the telehealth route is worth a real look tonight. Services like GoodRx Care or many state Medicaid lines can connect her with someone who can actually prescribe the right thing. That's the fastest path to her feeling better.
